mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.50% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% as of 2016-01-15 indicates deep easing cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.50% are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) relative to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 net new jobs, signaling labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100,000 persons
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite payroll deceleration, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which when combined produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The interaction amplifies the signal beyond either series alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 362,688M and HOUST at 549K as of 2016-01-15
failure_condition: Mean 20-day returns following joint RSAFS and HOUST declines are not significantly lower than returns following single-series declines (interaction F-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below its 3-month moving average, signaling supply-side weakness and reduced corporate capacity utilization that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite INDPRO weakness, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0%, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumption, which when combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) produces a dual confirmation of economic weakness and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.5% as of 2016-01-15 indicates elevated unemployment
regime_hint: post-crisis labor market weakness or recession onset
failure_condition: Mean 60-day returns following dual labor weakness are not significantly lower than returns in normal labor conditions (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) declines below 1.5% year-over-year, signaling deflationary pressure that increases real debt burdens and reduces nominal GDP growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days in a low-rate environment.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.60 and FEDFUNDS at 0.18% as of 2016-01-15
regime_hint: deflationary environment with zero lower bound constraints
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite low inflation and low rates, or Rank IC of the interaction < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,216.5B as of 2015-10-15 indicates modest growth trajectory
failure_condition: Mean 60-day returns in periods of sub-1.5% GDP growth are not significantly lower than returns in normal growth periods (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rise above 600,000 annualized units while retail sales (RSAFS) accelerate month-over-month, signaling robust consumer demand and housing market strength that supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 600,000 annualized AND RSAFS month-over-month change > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 549K and RSAFS at 362,688M as of 2016-01-15
failure_condition: Mean 20-day returns following joint housing and retail strength are not significantly higher than baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the interaction < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
